topic: Building India's first EdTech unicorn through offline community and brand
key_belief: Indian parents pay anything for their child's success. FOMO around competitive exams is the most powerful sales trigger in India. Brand visibility through cricket and celebrities creates instant trust with aspirational parents.
outcome: Reached 15 million users by 2019. First Indian EdTech unicorn. 5.7 billion dollar valuation. Proved Indian parents will pay premium for perceived quality education brand.
contradicts_western: Build product first, market later. In India, brand perception precedes product trial.
content: Byju's grew by sponsoring Indian cricket team jersey in 2019. Parents saw it during IPL and trusted it instantly. Celebrity endorsements with Shah Rukh Khan created mass aspiration. Offline BDA model converted leads aggressively. Early product was genuinely good. Growth came from understanding parental anxiety around IIT JEE and NEET. FOMO was the real product
